title: Repair related-test retrieval and compression evaluation
goal: Remove the live-goldset regression by incorporating retrieval-resolved test paths as an explicit non-graph evidence class, fixing path projection hygiene, and evaluating context compression without weakening recall or authority boundaries.

approval.note: Use an isolated Lenskit branch/worktree. Preserve the existing read-only and non-authoritative boundaries. Re-run the pre-registered live goldset before any promotion decision.

acceptance.assertion: Test-like repository paths from the already resolved query projection are emitted as related-test candidates with evidence_type=resolved_query and their citation/range metadata; they are never represented as graph edges, runtime dependencies or test coverage.

Graph-edge, symbol-index, resolved-query and heuristic candidates remain distinguishable, deterministic and precedence-ordered; stronger evidence does not erase weaker evidence classes unless an explicitly tested dedupe rule applies.
Evaluation path projections reject empty and non-repository-relative path values so context-size metrics contain only meaningful candidates.

The evaluator records aggregate and per-case context-path reduction. Utility may be established only with no recall regression and either the pre-existing recall-advantage threshold or at least 20 percent context reduction at equal-or-better recall; default_promoted remains false.

The same three pre-registered live cases are rerun on coherent bundles. Grabowski must recover tests/test_job_finalizer.py, no case may regress against baseline recall, and all commits, manifests, run ids and canonical digests are recorded.
RepoBrief remains read-only and produces no source, Git, patch, test, pull-request, snapshot or memory mutation and no review, coverage or merge authority.
Tests, all CI gates, full downloadable diff, head- and diff-bound self-review, mergeability and post-merge validation pass before verification.

metadata.outcome: The live regression is repaired. The fixed three-repository replay retained target recall 1.0 with no case regression and reduced aggregate context paths by 50 percent. The real Grabowski path tests/test_job_finalizer.py is recovered as resolved_query evidence. The surface remains opt-in and default promotion remains disabled.

verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-resolved-tests: The merged refinement emits test-like paths from source_citation_projection as resolved_query evidence with citation_id, source_range, range_status, derived authority and explicit non-graph/non-coverage semantics.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-evidence-order: Graph-edge and symbol-index evidence retain higher priority; resolved-query evidence is deterministic and stronger than heuristic fallback. Heuristics are suppressed only when resolved test evidence exists and remain available otherwise, with the suppression count reported.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-path-hygiene: Raw path segments are checked before PurePosixPath normalization. Empty, absolute, backslash, double-slash, dot and parent segments are rejected; the resolved P2 review includes regression tests for dot segments at the beginning, middle and end.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-compression-metric: The evaluator validates and deduplicates paths, records per-case and aggregate compression, blocks utility on any case recall regression, and keeps default_promoted false.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-live-rerun: The same fixed Lenskit, Grabowski and Weltgewebe cases were rerun on exact recorded commits and coherent bundles. Recall remained 1.0 in every case, the Grabowski test was recovered, aggregate path reduction was 50 percent, and repository, manifest, run and canonical identities are durably bound.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-boundary: The merged adapter remains read-only and introduces no Git, shell, patch, source, test, snapshot, PR, review, coverage, merge or memory authority.
verified_acceptance.rcga-v1-t003-merge-gates: The reviewed head passed the complete repository test suite and all required diagnostics. The 64133-byte binary product diff is SHA-256 bound, the self-review passed, PR #996 was squash-merged, and central code/evidence blob SHAs match the merged main tree.
